_note: Batch 7: 51 additional real, web-researched Israel programs gathered 2026-07-07, in response to a 100-program target. Reaching 100 would have required lowering the verification bar below what batches 1-6 held to, so this batch stops at 51: every candidate that cleared the bar of (a) its own working website and/or (b) a listing on a source this project already trusts (Masa Israel, Touro's Israel Option, Hebrew University's Rothberg International School field-school program). Sourced mainly from studyinisrael.org's and aigya.org's curated Anglo yeshiva/seminary directories (deeper coverage of a niche batches 5-6 had only lightly touched) plus biblicalarchaeology.org's active-dig list. Two entries carry a lower-confidence flag in their tags (yeshivas-birchas-mordechai: no dedicated website found; jewel-for-women: current site has an SSL certificate mismatch suggesting possible dormancy) -- kept in per explicit user approval to import as-is, but worth a human recheck before treating as fully current. Language audit: Machon Roni is the first program in the whole dataset whose overseas track is explicitly built around a non-English population (Spanish-speaking conversion candidates); it carries the new spanish-speaking tag (category: language) alongside its English-language general track. Everything else in this batch is English-primary. Roughly 50 more unverified Anglo-yeshiva/seminary names remain unmined in the source directories if a future batch wants to continue past this one.

mens_yeshivot.name: Machon Yaakov
mens_yeshivot.description: A highly selective yeshiva in Har Nof accepting only 18 students a year, ages 21-30, for one to two years of advanced, serious Torah study.

mens_yeshivot.name: Ohr Avraham
mens_yeshivot.description: A gap-year yeshiva in Beit Shemesh for English-speaking post-high-school men.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Orchos Chaim
mens_yeshivot.description: A gap-year yeshiva in Jerusalem for English-speaking post-high-school men.

mens_yeshivot.name: Bat Ayin Yeshiva
mens_yeshivot.description: A Beit Midrash and rabbinical-ordination (Smicha) track for men roughly mid-20s to early-30s, in the Breslov-influenced community of Bat Ayin, with housing and meals provided.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Simchat Shlomo
mens_yeshivot.description: A Nachlaot yeshiva founded in 2001 by Rav Sholom Brodt, teaching Gemara, Halacha, and Chassidut in a style and spirit inspired by Rabbi Shlomo Carlebach, with full and part-time tracks.

mens_yeshivot.name: Dvar Yerushalayim
mens_yeshivot.description: A Har Nof yeshiva teaching Jewish philosophy, ethics, Bible, and Hebrew from introductory through advanced levels, accepting students with minimal prior Jewish background.

mens_yeshivot.name: Chai Israel
mens_yeshivot.description: A program for 17-19 year olds combining thought-provoking classes, volunteering, touring, and Israeli culture, over a full nine-month academic year or a one-semester option.
mens_yeshivot.durationText: Full year (9 months) or one semester
mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact Rabbi Dani Y. Zwick directly.

mens_yeshivot.name: Aish HaTorah – Essentials Program
mens_yeshivot.description: A 28-day introductory-to-Judaism program in the Old City for ages 18-29, covering topics like practical spirituality and Genesis alongside science, priced as an all-inclusive package with round-trip airfare from New York, meals, housing, and classes; a shorter, more affordable track distinct from Aish HaTorah's longer Aish Gesher and Foundations programs.
mens_yeshivot.cost: $499 all-inclusive (airfare from NY, meals, housing, classes)

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Shalom Rav
mens_yeshivot.description: A nurturing, spiritually-oriented yeshiva in Tzfat led by Rabbi Avshi Weingot.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Toras Moshe (TOMO)
mens_yeshivot.description: An English-speaking Litvish Orthodox yeshiva founded in 1982 by Rabbi Moshe Meiselman for serious advanced Talmud students from English-speaking countries, with over 1,000 graduates; students study 10.5 hours daily across Talmud, Mussar, and Halacha.

mens_yeshivot.name: Heichal Yerushalayim
mens_yeshivot.description: A Sephardic community yeshiva for post-high-school overseas students.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Toras Chaim
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva founded by the Kreiswirth family of Antwerp for overseas students who intend to complete their undergraduate degree, offering college-credit coordination including with Touro's Israel Option.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Ohr Yerushalayim
mens_yeshivot.description: A Beit Midrash program on Moshav Beit Meir in the Judean hills for English-speaking men from the US and other Anglo countries wanting a year or more of intensive study.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Chofetz Chaim Yerushalayim
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva teaching and inspiring students in Torah and mussar for over 55 years, carrying forward the mesorah of Slabodka.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Ma'ale Gilboa
mens_yeshivot.description: A Shiluv yeshiva combining two years of Torah study with the full three years of IDF service (rather than the shorter Hesder track), emphasizing rigorous study alongside intellectual openness and social consciousness; its overseas program is fully integrated with the Israeli program, adding English-language classes on top of the Hebrew ones, and draws post-high-school and post-college students from the US, UK, and Australia.
mens_yeshivot.durationText: 2 years of study integrated with a 3-year IDF service track

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Nesivos Ahron
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva founded in 2008 to provide motivated young men with the foundations of Torah and avodas Hashem.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Zeev Hatorah
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva and learning community dedicated to Torah study alongside a heightened sense of individual and communal responsibility.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Darchei Yonatan
mens_yeshivot.description: An academically-oriented yeshiva in Efrat named in memory of Rabbi Jonathan Sacks, former Chief Rabbi of the United Hebrew Congregations of the Commonwealth.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshiva Gedolah Yagdil Torah
mens_yeshivot.description: A Jerusalem yeshiva for post-high-school men, operating as a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Bais Yisroel
mens_yeshivot.description: An English-language Litvish Orthodox yeshiva founded in 1985 by Rabbi Doniel Lehrfield, with over 100 students from the US, England, Canada, South Africa, Mexico, Chile, and Australia.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Birchas Mordechai
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva in Beitar led by Rav Yaakov Friedman.
mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly; no dedicated website was found during research, so verify current status before relying on this entry.

mens_yeshivot.name: Chasedei Breslov Yeshiva
mens_yeshivot.description: An English-speaking kollel and yeshiva, running since 1987, teaching Rebbe Nachman's Breslov teachings on faith, prayer, and Torah, with a full in-person track and an International Remote Study option.

mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Ohr David
mens_yeshivot.description: A yeshiva founded in 1980 by Rabbi Chaim Flom for growth-minded students, drawing from communities around the United States, with trips throughout Israel including the Golan Heights and Eilat.

womens_seminaries.name: She'arim
womens_seminaries.description: A Har Nof seminary founded in 1994 with about 50 students a year, offering morning one-on-one learning and afternoon lectures on Chumash, Navi, and tefilla, plus a short introductory "Taste of Torah" track for women new to their heritage.

womens_seminaries.name: Baer Miriam
womens_seminaries.description: A Har Nof seminary for women at all learning levels, with personal teacher involvement and challenging classes.

womens_seminaries.name: Tomer Devorah Seminary
womens_seminaries.description: A seminary opened in 2006 with a dual focus on building textual/learning skills and character, offering Bible, Prophets, Proverbs, Law, and Philosophy classes with dorm housing and mentoring.

womens_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Drisha (Shana Alef)
womens_seminaries.description: A one-year program for highly motivated post-high-school women at the Drisha Institute's flagship Israel yeshiva (opened 2018, Shana Alef track added 2020), offering intensive Talmud Torah alongside advanced learners; overseas students, about a third of the cohort, are fully integrated with Israeli students in a Hebrew-speaking environment with English-speaking staff support.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Drisha Institute website.

womens_seminaries.name: Neve Yerushalayim – Mechina Introductory Program
womens_seminaries.description: A 6-week introductory track at Neve Yerushalayim for women ages 19-35, featuring prominent lecturers on philosophical and theological topics, private tutorials, campus tours, and dorm housing, with financial aid available; a shorter taste of Neve's curriculum distinct from the school's two longer programs already in this database.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Neve Yerushalayim website.

womens_seminaries.name: Mayanot Women's Program
womens_seminaries.description: A Judaic Studies and Yeshiva track for women ages 20-29 with discussion-style classes, small class sizes, and no dress code or religious commitment required; the women's counterpart to Mayanot's men's post-high-school program already in this database.

womens_seminaries.name: Meorot Yerushalayim
womens_seminaries.description: The only gap-year seminary geared exclusively to women who attended public school or grew up in non-observant homes, run by Rabbi Yaakov Lynn with 20-30 students a year and individualized attention; about a quarter of graduates go on to Touro or Stern College.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Meorot Yerushalayim website.

womens_seminaries.name: Midreshet Ein Hanatziv
womens_seminaries.description: A Beit Midrash on a working northern-Israel kibbutz especially suited to women preparing for IDF service, though open to all, with classes spanning Tanakh, Talmud, Jewish law history, philosophy, Chassidut, and gender in Judaism; overseas students are fully immersed with 60-80 Israeli students living on the same schedule.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Midreshet Ein Hanatziv website.

womens_seminaries.name: Midreshet B'erot Bat Ayin
womens_seminaries.description: Israel's only Holistic Jewish Women's Seminary, combining intensive Tanach, Halacha, and Chassidut study with creative spiritual expression through dance, art, drama, writing, and music, at beginner through advanced levels; also runs a B'not Ruth conversion track for women becoming Jewish. Founded and directed by Rebbetzin Chana Bracha Siegelbaum.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Midreshet B'erot Bat Ayin website.

womens_seminaries.name: Machon Roni
womens_seminaries.description: A women's Torah institute affiliated with Diaspora Yeshiva, teaching women seeking conversion to Judaism, with a track conducted primarily in Spanish for Spanish-speaking conversion candidates alongside its general English-language track.

womens_seminaries.name: Machon Raaya
womens_seminaries.description: A Har Nof seminary known for being warm and accepting of every student, with rigorous, intellectually in-depth learning; a participating seminary in Touro's and Hebrew Theological College's Israel Option programs.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply directly through the seminary, or via a participating overseas college's Israel Option.

womens_seminaries.name: Ateres Bnos Yerushalayim
womens_seminaries.description: A seminary founded 24 years ago by Rabbi Hillel Belsky, located near the Kosel, drawing on his decades of experience in girls' chinuch; a participating seminary in Touro's Israel Option.

womens_seminaries.name: Chemdas Bais Yaakov
womens_seminaries.description: A Jerusalem seminary and participating school in Touro's Israel Option; no dedicated website was found during research, so verify current status before relying on this entry.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply via a participating overseas college's Israel Option, or contact the seminary directly.

womens_seminaries.name: Machon Sara Yerushalayim (MSY)
womens_seminaries.description: A Bais Yaakov-track seminary for post-high-school women.

womens_seminaries.name: Bnos Sarah Seminary
womens_seminaries.description: A one-year teacher-training program for recent English-speaking Bais Yaakov high school graduates with strong halachic observance and academics; students average above an 86% GPA and come mostly from North America and Europe, with 80% of classes taught in Hebrew to build language skills.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the Masa Israel program page.

womens_seminaries.name: Bnos Chava Seminary
womens_seminaries.description: A one-year teacher-training program for recent Bais Yaakov high school graduates.

womens_seminaries.name: Rinas Bais Yaakov
womens_seminaries.description: A highly academic Bais Yaakov seminary with high-caliber, renowned teachers and an extensive schedule of trips and tours.
womens_seminaries.signupInstructions: Apply through the seminary's own site or the Masa Israel program page.

coed_programs.name: Isralight
coed_programs.description: A flexible-length Jewish learning program in the Old City for all ages, running anywhere from half a day to a few weeks.
coed_programs.durationText: Half a day to a few weeks

coed_programs.name: Bnei Akiva Olamit: Shalem
coed_programs.description: A year of chesed (Sherut Leumi / National Service) for girls from around the world through World Bnei Akiva, described as the only real National Service program for overseas girls; participants join the Bat-Ami national-service network with placements at Beit Meir Hospital or the ALUT center for children with autism, alongside Torah classes, seminars, and touring. A sixth distinct World Bnei Akiva program track alongside Kadima, Torani, MTA, Limmud, and Mechina Olamit, all already in this database.

coed_programs.name: JEWEL for Women
coed_programs.description: A short, inexpensive introduction to Judaism in Jerusalem for ages 19-31, with airfare, room, board, classes, and field trips included; taught in English with no Hebrew required. Current website shows a certificate mismatch as of this research, so verify the program is still actively running before relying on this entry.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Tel Megiddo Excavation
archaeology_field_schools.description: A summer excavation exploring Middle Bronze city gates, an undiscovered Iron Age palace, and the archive of Late Bronze Age kings, with participants housed at Mishmar HaEmek guest facilities; the fee is negotiable and covers housing, meals, and daily transportation, and 3-6 academic credits are available through Tel Aviv University.
archaeology_field_schools.cost: Negotiable; housing, meals and transport included
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Contact the Megiddo Expedition, or apply via Tel Aviv University for credit.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Tel Abel Beth Maacah Excavation
archaeology_field_schools.description: A summer field school continuing excavation of Iron Age II contexts to investigate whether the city was part of the Israelite Kingdom, directed by Naama Yahalom-Mack and Nava Panitz-Cohen; participants can join for the field experience alone or add credit through Hebrew University's Rothberg International School.
archaeology_field_schools.durationText: Summer field school season, dates vary by year
archaeology_field_schools.cost: Field fee plus optional $120 application fee and $120 per credit for Rothberg credit
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through the excavation's official website.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Tel Gezer Excavation
archaeology_field_schools.organization: Nelson Glueck School of Biblical Archaeology
archaeology_field_schools.description: An excavation at Tel Gezer, one of the first sites in Israel to use student volunteers instead of paid laborers and the first to offer a field school for students; fees and academic credit vary by sponsoring university program.
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through a sponsoring university's study-abroad office or the Nelson Glueck School directly.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Hazor Excavations
archaeology_field_schools.organization: Hazor Excavations, Hebrew University of Jerusalem
archaeology_field_schools.location: Kibbutz Kfar HaNassi, near Tel Hazor
archaeology_field_schools.description: A summer field school at Hazor, the largest biblical-era city in Israel and a major political, economic and cultural center for over a millennium; participants stay at Kibbutz Kfar HaNassi on the banks of the Jordan River, with credit available (up to 1 credit per week) through Hebrew University's Rothberg International School.
archaeology_field_schools.cost: Field fee plus optional $120 application fee and $120 per credit for Rothberg credit
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through the excavation's official website; payment due by May 1, 2026 for the 2026 season.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Shiloh Excavations
archaeology_field_schools.description: A spring excavation open to volunteers of any experience level, offered in flexible one- to five-week blocks, with the first four weeks focused on digging and the fifth on digging and restoration.
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through Associates for Biblical Research's website.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Hippos-Sussita Excavations
archaeology_field_schools.description: A four-week excavation with field work in the mornings (5:00am-12:00pm, Sunday-Thursday) and lab work activities in the afternoons.
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through the Hippos Excavations Project website.

archaeology_field_schools.name: Tel Akko Total Archaeology Project
archaeology_field_schools.organization: Penn State University / University of Haifa
archaeology_field_schools.description: A summer field school running since 2010, led by Dr. Ann Killebrew (Penn State) and Dr. Michal Artzy (University of Haifa), taking a 'total archaeology' approach spanning excavation, survey, GIS, on-site conservation, underwater archaeology, and community outreach.
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Apply through the Tel Akko Total Archaeology Project website.

archaeology_field_schools.name: En-Gedi Oasis Archaeological Project
archaeology_field_schools.description: A newly launched excavation under Hebrew University's Institute of Archaeology, led by Dr. Uri Davidovich, with participants staying at a glamping-style resort within Kibbutz En-Gedi; one credit per week of excavation is available from Hebrew University.
archaeology_field_schools.signupInstructions: Contact the excavation directly for the current season's dates.
